cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Arrhenius acid
cards.answer: A substance that produces $\text{H}^+$ (or $\text{H}_3\text{O}^+$) when dissolved in water

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Arrhenius base
cards.answer: A substance that produces $\text{OH}^-$ when dissolved in water

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Brønsted-Lowry acid

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Brønsted-Lowry base

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Lewis acid

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: Lewis base

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\text{pH} = -\log[\text{H}_3\text{O}^+]$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\text{pOH} = -\log[\text{OH}^-]$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\text{pH} + \text{pOH} = 14$ at 25°C

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $K_w = [\text{H}_3\text{O}^+][\text{OH}^-] = 1.0 \times 10^{-14}$ at 25°C

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: An acid that completely dissociates in water (e.g., $\text{HCl}$, $\text{HNO}_3$, $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$, $\text{HBr}$, $\text{HI}$, $\text{HClO}_4$)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: An acid that only partially dissociates in water; has an equilibrium characterized by $K_a$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: For weak acid $\text{HA} \rightleftharpoons \text{H}^+ + \text{A}^-$: $K_a = \frac{[\text{H}^+][\text{A}^-]}{[\text{HA}]}$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $K_a \times K_b = K_w = 1.0 \times 10^{-14}$ for a conjugate acid-base pair at 25°C

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The species formed after an acid donates a proton; differs by one $\text{H}^+$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The species formed after a base accepts a proton; differs by one $\text{H}^+$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A solution that resists changes in pH when small amounts of acid or base are added

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: $\text{pH} = \text{p}K_a + \log\frac{[\text{A}^-]}{[\text{HA}]}$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The point during acid-base titration where pH = pKa and [HA] = [A⁻]

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: At the equivalence point of a strong acid–strong base titration, the pH is...
cards.answer: Exactly 7.0, because only water and a neutral salt (e.g., $\text{NaCl}$) are present

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: At the equivalence point of a weak acid–strong base titration, the pH is...
cards.answer: Greater than 7, because the conjugate base hydrolyzes to produce $\text{OH}^-$

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: An acid or base capable of donating or accepting multiple protons (e.g., $\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4$, $\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4$)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: A molecule that can act as both an acid and a base (e.g., $\text{H}_2\text{O}$, $\text{HCO}_3^-$)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: As $K_a$ increases for a series of weak acids, the acid strength...
cards.answer: Increases (larger $K_a$ = more dissociation = stronger weak acid)

cards.subject: ap-chemistry
cards.prompt: The measure of how much acid or base a buffer can absorb before the pH changes significantly
